title: Figure 68 from: Grishin N, Cong Q (2014) A new Hermeuptychia (Lepidoptera, Nymphalidae, Satyrinae) is sympatric and synchronic with H. sosybius in southeast US coastal plains, while another new Hermeuptychia species – not hermes – inhabits south Texas and northeast Mexico. ZooKeys 379: 43-91. https://doi.org/10.3897/zookeys.379.6394
author: Cong, Qian
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 68 - DNA-barcoded Hermeuptychia specimens from USA: Texas: Fort Bend Co., Brazos Bend State Park. Hermeuptychia intricata is above the line and Hermeuptychia sosybius is below the line, photographed prior to removal of body parts for DNA extraction. DNA voucher codes (see Table 1 for data) are shown below each specimen. Hypothetical field marks are indicated on the first specimen of each species. NVG-1537–NVG-1553 are from Horseshoe Lake trail, 29°22'54.96", −95°36'41.06", 15 m; and NVG-1554–NVG-1567 are from near Hale Lake, 29°22'48.27", −95°35'05.02", 16 m, all collected on 17-Aug-2013. Both species are present in each locality. Images are scaled approximately. "F" specifies mirror image (left-right inverted).
